subject: A hooded outcast figure perched in a thorny bramble tree, glowering downward at something below
description: A robed and hooded human figure crouches in the crook of a dead, thorn-choked tree, gripping a branch with one hand and glaring down at an unseen target below. The figure wears a layered cloak in muted purples and browns over what appears to be ragged adventurer's gear; their face is partly shadowed by the hood, mouth set in a sour scowl. Dense black brambles and bare branches fill the frame around them, with a few sickly greenish leaves clinging to the wood. The background is a near-black void of overlapping thorns. The flavor text identifies the speaker as Terryn, an Edgewall outcast, observing a mob — placing the figure as the observer rather than the prey.
foreground: hooded outcast clutching a thorny branch, surrounded by brambles
background: near-black tangle of thorny branches receding into shadow
emotion: scowling, watchful, resentful — perched-predator body language
